reportText: @ Canyon Mouth , 188 cfs @ Fort Collins , 1 cfs @ North Fork below Seaman Reservoir Flows have leveled, and it is running clear all throughout the canyon. With the colder weather moving through the canyon water temperatures are still quite cold. Looks like an early, light terrestrial bite is starting to show up. Consider running a dry-dropper rig in the afternoons on warmer days. Keep things on the smaller side fishing size 12-18 ants, beetles, and smaller hopper patterns. With spring moving in on the Poudre, bug diversity is improving. Larger Stonefly and caddis imitations have been finding success. With the colder weather this week, nymphing has found the most success of the water. Smaller blue wing, midge's, and stonefly patterns have been producing. Spring is definitely starting to show its face around here, and we’re getting more guide trips out on the Poudre. Give us a call to get the lowdown! Take a look at our Poudre Assortment , a great selection for this time of year.
